task: Write a Python function weighted_mean(values, weights) returning the weighted arithmetic mean as a float. Empty lists return None, and a zero total weight also returns None, never a crash. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The function accurately calculates the weighted mean, handles empty lists and zero total weight cases correctly, and meets all coding standards.
trace: The function first checks if the input lists are empty or have a zero total weight, in which case it returns `None`. Then it calculates the total of the weighted values and divides by the total weight to get the weighted arithmetic mean. To handle an edge case, the function uses zip() to iterate over both lists simultaneously while keeping their order.
